data.title: a16z Urges SEC for Token Issuance Guidance to Retain Blockchain Ownership
data.description: According to Odaily, venture capital firm a16z has called on the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide clear guidance on token issuance. During a meeting with the SEC's cryptocurrency task force, led by Commissioner Hester Peirce, a16z emphasized that such guidance could prevent the trend of issuing tokens exclusively to non-U.S. citizens. This trend, they argue, effectively transfers the ownership of U.S.-developed blockchain technology overseas.

data.title: a16z: If the SEC provides guidance on token issuance, it can prevent the transfer of ownership of blockchain technology developed in the United States to overseas
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News a16z said at the cryptocurrency working group meeting of Hester Peirce, Commissioner of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission: "If the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission can provide guidance on token issuance, it will prevent the trend of token issuance only to non-U.S. citizens, which is actually transferring the ownership of blockchain technology developed in the United States overseas."

data.title: He Yi: Binance will adopt a new voting mechanism for the next "vote to delist"
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Binance co-founder He Yi said on the X platform that "the voting to delist coins is for the project parties that are about to delist coins to buy votes for others", "Binance's next voting to delist will be for the tokens that no one chooses."

data.title: Montana proposes legislation to strengthen digital asset regulation; fraud losses in the United States will exceed $12.5 billion in 2024
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Montana is accelerating the digital asset regulatory framework to cope with the surge in cryptocurrency fraud. State Securities and Insurance Commissioner James Brown cited data from the Federal Trade Commission, saying that fraud losses in the United States will reach $12.5 billion in 2024, a year-on-year increase of 25%. Among them, the elderly group has become the main victim because of its sixth largest population in the United States. 
Brown supports the "Network Token Regulation Act" considered by the state legislature. The proposal will authorize regulators to implement access review and continuous supervision of blockchain trading service providers. At the same time, if the bill is passed, Montana will become the first jurisdiction in the United States to systematically regulate on-chain trading entities. (Bitcoin.com)

data.title: Montana Advances Digital Asset Regulation Amid Rising Crypto Fraud
data.description:
According to Odaily, Montana is accelerating the development of a regulatory framework for digital assets in response to a surge in cryptocurrency fraud. State Securities and Insurance Commissioner James Brown cited data from the Federal Trade Commission, revealing that nationwide fraud losses reached $12.5 billion in 2024, marking a 25% increase from the previous year. The elderly population, which ranks sixth in the nation by proportion, has been identified as a primary target for these scams.

Brown is advocating for the state legislature to review the proposed 'Digital Token Regulation Act.' This legislation would empower regulatory bodies to conduct entry reviews and ongoing supervision of blockchain transaction service providers. If enacted, Montana would become the first U.S. jurisdiction to systematically regulate on-chain transaction entities.

data.title: FBI Issues ‘Medusa’ Alert As Hackers Target Critical Infrastructure, Extort Victims for Cash
data.description:
The FBI is issuing an alert over an ongoing ransomware campaign known as “Medusa” that’s hit hundreds of victims. Medusa is a type of malicious software first discovered in 2021 that encrypts its victims’ files before demanding a ransom in exchange for a decryption key..
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2027/03/22/fbi-issues-medusa-alert-as-hackers-target-critical-infrastructure-extort-victims-for-cash
